HMW-V03: Driver Assignment Visibility in Job List

How might we show the dispatcher which FM jobs already have a driver assigned, so they can scan the job list and quickly spot "which jobs still need a driver" without opening each one?
Context: The vendor dispatcher opens My Jobs each morning with ~10-20 jobs. Some are FM Trucking (need a driver), others are EC/CS/CR/OH (no driver needed). Among the FM jobs, some already have a driver assigned from yesterday, some are new and need assignment. The dispatcher's first task is: "scan the list, find the unassigned FM jobs, assign drivers." This scan needs to be fast. The question is how much driver info belongs in the list vs the detail page.

Option A: Dedicated "Driver" Column

Most Info
Add a "Driver" column to the table. FM jobs show the assigned driver name (or "—" if unassigned). Non-FM services show "—" since they don't have drivers. This gives the most information at a glance but adds a column to an already-dense table.
Pros
• Dispatcher sees driver name without clicking into the job
• Can answer "which jobs is Zhang Wei on today?" by scanning one column
• Unassigned FM jobs are obvious ("—" in the driver column)
Cons
• Adds an 8th column to a 7-column table. At 768px this breaks the condensed layout.
• Non-FM jobs show "—" in this column, wasting space for EC/CS/CR/OH vendors
• Table becomes wider than admin Jobs page, breaking "same system" feel
• HaleSun does FM + OH. Only FM rows use the column. 40-60% of rows show "—".
Teleport OS Vendor My Jobs Fleet HaleSun
Active 8 Completed 3 Verified 5 All 18
Shipment Customer Svc Route Pickup Driver Status Cost
DO-2603-001 TikTok FM TikTok WH, Shenzhen → SZX Airport 30 Mar
08:00		 Zhang Wei Pending MYR 310
DO-2603-003 Shopee FM Yantian Port → Shenzhen Airport 30 Mar
10:00		 — Pending MYR 520
DO-2603-002 Shopee CR Yantian Port 30 Mar
09:00		 — In Progress CNY 400
DO-2603-005 Shein OH SZX Airport Cargo Terminal 30 Mar
14:00		 — Pending MYR 338
DO-2603-007 Temu FM Qianhai FTZ → Huanggang Port 30 Mar
15:00		 Li Ming In Progress MYR 480

Option B: Small Icon Indicator Next to Status

Balanced
No new column. Instead, show a small person icon next to the status chip for FM jobs that have a driver assigned. Unassigned FM jobs show a faded/empty person icon. Non-FM jobs show nothing. Hover the icon to see driver name + plate in a tooltip. This keeps the table at 7 columns while still letting the dispatcher scan for unassigned jobs.
Pros
• Table stays at 7 columns, no width breakage
• Unassigned FM jobs visually distinct (faded icon vs blue icon)
• Non-FM services unaffected (no icon, no wasted space)
• Works at 768px condensed layout without changes
Cons
• Can't see driver NAME without hovering or clicking
• Icon is small (14px), might be missed by some users
• Hover tooltips don't work on tablet (touch devices)
Teleport OS Vendor My Jobs Fleet HaleSun

14:00		 Pending MYR 338
DO-2603-007 Temu FM Qianhai FTZ → Huanggang Port 30 Mar
15:00		 In Progress MYR 480

Option C: Driver Name as Sub-line Under Customer

Recommended
No new column, no icon. For FM jobs with a driver assigned, show the driver name as a small sub-line under the customer name (same cell, stacked). Unassigned FM jobs show "No driver" in faint text. Non-FM jobs show nothing under the customer name. This uses the existing Customer column's vertical space and avoids adding visual elements to the Status column, which should stay focused on status.
Pros
• Driver name visible without hover or click
• No new column, table stays at 7 columns
• Status column stays clean (status only)
• Sub-line pattern is already used in the table (shipment DO# under customer)
• Works on tablet and touch devices (no hover dependency)
• "No driver" faint text makes unassigned FM jobs scannable
Cons
• Customer cell gets taller for FM jobs (2 lines vs 1 for non-FM)
• Driver info is coupled with customer info spatially, which is semantically odd
• If the sub-line already shows DO# below customer, adding driver makes it 3 lines
Teleport OS Vendor My Jobs Fleet HaleSun

FM Qianhai FTZ → Huanggang Port 30 Mar
15:00		 In Progress MYR 480

Option D: Driver + Vehicle as Sub-line Under Route

Recommended
Show driver name + vehicle plate as a small sub-line under the route text. The route column has the most horizontal space (35%) and the driver/vehicle is semantically linked to the route, not the customer. The driver IS the person executing that route. Unassigned FM jobs show "No driver assigned" in faint text. Non-FM jobs show nothing.
Pros
• Semantic fit: driver + vehicle belong to the route, not the customer
• Route column has the most width (35%), plenty of room for a sub-line
• Driver name + plate visible without hover or click (works on tablets)
• No new column, table stays at 7 columns
• "No driver assigned" in faint text makes unassigned FM jobs instantly scannable
• At 768px condensed layout, Route column is dropped anyway, so sub-line info moves to detail page naturally
Cons
• Route cell gets taller for FM jobs (route text + driver sub-line)
• Route text is already truncated with ellipsis. Adding a second line means more vertical space
Teleport OS Vendor My Jobs Fleet HaleSun

DO-2603-007 Temu FM
Qianhai FTZ → Huanggang Port
Li Ming · 粤B·67890
30 Mar
15:00		 In Progress MYR 480

Recommendation: Option D — Driver + Vehicle Sub-line Under Route

Option D wins on semantic fit. The driver and vehicle are executing the ROUTE, not serving the customer. Placing "Zhang Wei · 粤B·12345" under "TikTok WH → SZX Airport" reads naturally: here's where the cargo goes, and here's who's taking it there.

The route column has the most horizontal space (35%), so the sub-line fits without cramping. Blue text for assigned drivers, faint gray "No driver assigned" for unassigned FM jobs. Non-FM services (CR, OH, EC, CS) show nothing extra. At 768px the Route column is already dropped per HMW-V01, so the driver info naturally moves to the detail page.

Option A (column) wastes space for non-FM. Option B (icon) requires hover. Option C (under Customer) has weak semantic connection. Option D uses the widest column and the strongest semantic link.
